heading: Internationalization with next-intl
body: The guide uses next-intl for internationalization. The routing configuration defines supported locales (currently just `en`) with `localePrefix: 'as-needed'` so English URLs do not have a `/en/` prefix. Translation strings live in `frontend/src/messages/en.json` and are loaded server-side by the request config. The `NextIntlClientProvider` in the locale layout makes translations available to all client components via the `useTranslations` hook.
